author: jsign
content:
Sharing some new stuff that I think might be interested for some teams:
- We have a Ethrex+Prysm (combination isn't relevant as far as EL witness gen conforms to spec) combo joined `glamsterdam-devnet-5` and a new tooling that automatically generates `statelessInputBytes` for all blocks from that devnet. These are automatically uploaded to an [public R2 bucket](https://pub-5345007fbd06486bbb7cbbe9f3112c45.r2.dev/devnets/glamsterdam-devnet-5/index.html).
- The index page has some meta files that can help automation in discovering artifacts. e.g. for now we upload batches of 10 blocks with required `statelessInputBytes` + other metadata.
- Goal here is to provide public dataset of live networks complying to latest active spec of `projects/zkevm` on top of active devnet, which today is [tests-zkevm@v0.4.1](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/releases/tag/tests-zkevm%40v0.4.1).
- This means that you can use EEST fixtures in releases as tests (which have the `statelessInputBytes`), and now with this extra pipeline also the same required data to run real blocks from latest `glamsterdam-devnet-x` (`x==5` now, but the idea is to keep on top of the latest one, which is aligned with `tests-zkevm@vX.X.X` release strategy).
- On top of that, we have a cron-CI every 2hs pulling the latest 10 batches from the R2 bucket and running literally the block statelessly into the corresponding `execution-specs` code for `tests-zkevm@v0.4.1` (or whatever is the latest)).
- You can see example of those CI runs [here](https://github.com/eth-act/zkevm-benchmark-workload/actions/runs/27707375666). Or more generally [here](https://github.com/eth-act/zkevm-benchmark-workload/actions).
- The way to interpret what the CI is doing: the CI is checking that every `glamsterdam-devnet-5` block `statelessInputBytes` conforming to the specs should pass executed in the stateless block validator in the spec; so things are coherent. So "real blocks" also are coherent apart from EEST tests.
